name: task_automation_v1
description: Evaluates two task-automation workflows optimised for CRON / WebHook triggers: (1) a competitive-research pipeline that gathers data, scores competitors, and routes to different report formats based on findings, and (2) a document-processing pipeline that ingests files, classifies them, extracts fields, validates, and routes to approval or rejection. Both workflows exercise 50+ agentAction states with deep conditional branching.
workflow_spec:
Build a workflow named `competitive_research` that runs a full competitive-research and document-processing pipeline end-to-end. This is designed to be triggered by a CRON job or WebHook with initial-args. The workflow MUST contain at least the following steps as separate agentAction states. Every branch lives as `conditions` inside the owning agentAction — no separate `choice` states.

PHASE 1 — RESEARCH INTAKE (steps 1–8):
1. receive_trigger — acknowledge the trigger source and log the run timestamp. Variable: trigger_source (string, one of: cron, webhook, manual).
2. check_trigger_type — if trigger_source is 'manual', branch to `request_research_topic`; otherwise continue to load_config.
3. load_config — load the research configuration (target industry, competitor list, date range). Variables: industry (string), competitor_count (number), date_range_days (number).
4. validate_config — if competitor_count is less than 1, branch to `abort_invalid_config`; if competitor_count is greater than 20, branch to `abort_too_many_competitors`; otherwise continue.
5. request_research_topic — ask the user for the research topic and industry (only reached from manual trigger). Variable: research_topic (string).
6. set_research_scope — determine whether the research scope is narrow (competitor_count <= 3), medium (4–10), or broad (> 10). Variable: research_scope (string).
7. check_scope_resources — if research_scope is 'broad' and date_range_days is greater than 90, branch to `warn_large_scope`; otherwise continue.
8. confirm_parameters — summarise all parameters and confirm the run should proceed. Variable: parameters_confirmed (boolean).

PHASE 2 — DATA GATHERING (steps 9–16):
9. check_parameters_confirmed — if parameters_confirmed is false, branch to `abort_user_cancelled`; otherwise continue.
10. gather_web_data — search the web for recent news, press releases, and blog posts about each competitor. Variable: web_results_count (number).
11. check_web_results — if web_results_count is 0, branch to `no_web_data_fallback`; otherwise continue.
12. gather_financial_data — pull publicly available financial data (revenue estimates, funding rounds, headcount). Variable: financial_data_available (boolean).
13. check_financial_data — if financial_data_available is false, branch to `skip_financial_analysis`; otherwise continue.
14. gather_social_signals — collect social-media mention counts, sentiment scores, and trending topics. Variable: social_sentiment (string, one of: positive, neutral, negative, mixed).
15. gather_product_updates — check for product launches, feature announcements, and pricing changes. Variable: product_updates_count (number).
16. aggregate_raw_data — combine all gathered data into a single raw dataset. Variable: total_data_points (number).

PHASE 3 — ANALYSIS & SCORING (steps 17–28):
17. check_data_sufficiency — if total_data_points is less than 10, branch to `insufficient_data_report`; otherwise continue.
18. score_market_position — assign each competitor a market-position score 1–100. Variable: avg_market_score (number).
19. score_innovation — assign each competitor an innovation score based on product updates and patents. Variable: avg_innovation_score (number).
20. score_financial_health — assign each competitor a financial-health score. Variable: avg_financial_score (number).
21. compute_threat_level — compute a composite threat index from the three scores. Variable: threat_level (string, one of: low, medium, high, critical).
22. check_threat_level — if threat_level is 'critical', branch to `escalate_critical_threat`; if threat_level is 'high', branch to `flag_high_threat`; otherwise continue.
23. analyse_sentiment_trends — identify whether sentiment is trending up, down, or stable. Variable: sentiment_trend (string, one of: improving, declining, stable).
24. check_sentiment_decline — if sentiment_trend is 'declining' and social_sentiment is 'negative', branch to `flag_negative_sentiment`; otherwise continue.
25. identify_opportunities — list market gaps or weaknesses in competitors. Variable: opportunities_found (number).
26. identify_risks — list competitive risks or threats. Variable: risks_found (number).
27. check_risk_ratio — if risks_found is greater than opportunities_found, branch to `generate_defensive_report`; otherwise continue.
28. generate_swot_summary — produce a SWOT (Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats) summary.

PHASE 4 — DOCUMENT PROCESSING (steps 29–40):
29. receive_documents — receive uploaded documents for processing. Variable: document_count (number).
30. check_document_count — if document_count is 0, branch to `no_documents_provided`; if document_count is greater than 50, branch to `too_many_documents`; otherwise continue.
31. classify_documents — classify each document by type (report, invoice, contract, correspondence, other). Variable: primary_doc_type (string).
32. check_doc_type — if primary_doc_type is 'contract', branch to `extract_contract_fields`; if primary_doc_type is 'invoice', branch to `extract_invoice_fields`; otherwise continue to extract_general_fields.
33. extract_general_fields — extract title, date, author, and key entities from general documents. Variable: extraction_confidence (number, 0–100).
34. extract_contract_fields — extract parties, effective date, expiration date, value, and key clauses. Variable: extraction_confidence (number, 0–100).
35. extract_invoice_fields — extract vendor, amount, due date, line items, and PO number. Variable: extraction_confidence (number, 0–100).
36. validate_extraction — if extraction_confidence is less than 60, branch to `flag_low_confidence`; if extraction_confidence is greater than or equal to 60 and less than 85, branch to `request_human_review`; otherwise continue.
37. check_duplicates — check if any extracted document is a duplicate of an existing record. Variable: has_duplicates (boolean).
38. check_duplicate_action — if has_duplicates is true, branch to `handle_duplicates`; otherwise continue.
39. enrich_metadata — add tags, categories, and cross-references to extracted data. Variable: enrichment_complete (boolean).
40. check_enrichment — if enrichment_complete is false, branch to `enrichment_failed`; otherwise continue.

PHASE 5 — REPORT GENERATION & DELIVERY (steps 41–50):
41. select_report_format — determine output format based on trigger. Variable: report_format (string, one of: pdf, markdown, json, email).
42. check_report_format — if report_format is 'email', branch to `compose_email_report`; if report_format is 'json', branch to `generate_json_output`; otherwise continue to generate_document_report.
43. generate_document_report — produce a formatted PDF or Markdown report with executive summary, competitor profiles, SWOT, and recommendations.
44. compose_email_report — compose a concise email summary with key findings and attach the full report.
45. generate_json_output — produce a structured JSON payload for downstream API consumption.
46. attach_processed_documents — attach all processed document extractions to the report.
47. determine_distribution — decide the distribution list based on threat level. Variable: distribution_scope (string, one of: team, leadership, board).
48. check_distribution_scope — if distribution_scope is 'board', branch to `add_executive_summary`; otherwise continue.
49. add_executive_summary — prepend a one-page executive summary for board-level distribution.
50. deliver_report — send the report to all recipients and log the delivery.

TERMINAL & ERROR STATES (steps 51–62):
51. abort_invalid_config — report that the configuration is invalid (competitor_count < 1) and terminate.
52. abort_too_many_competitors — report that too many competitors were specified (> 20) and terminate.
53. abort_user_cancelled — report that the run was cancelled by the user.
54. warn_large_scope — warn that the scope is very large and may take extended time, then continue to confirm_parameters.
55. no_web_data_fallback — report that no web data was found and fall back to financial and social data only, then continue to gather_financial_data.
56. skip_financial_analysis — skip financial scoring and continue to gather_social_signals.
57. insufficient_data_report — generate a minimal report noting insufficient data and terminate.
58. escalate_critical_threat — escalate immediately to leadership with an urgent alert and terminate.
59. flag_high_threat — flag the report as high-priority and continue to analyse_sentiment_trends.
60. flag_negative_sentiment — add a sentiment-decline warning section to the report and continue to identify_opportunities.
61. generate_defensive_report — generate a defensive-strategy report instead of the standard one and proceed to deliver_report.
62. no_documents_provided — report that no documents were provided for processing and skip to select_report_format.
63. too_many_documents — report that document batch exceeds the 50-document limit and terminate.
64. flag_low_confidence — flag documents with low extraction confidence for manual processing and terminate.
65. request_human_review — queue documents for human review and continue to check_duplicates.
66. handle_duplicates — merge or skip duplicate documents and continue to enrich_metadata.
67. enrichment_failed — report enrichment failure and continue to select_report_format with partial data.
68. add_executive_summary_done — (convergence after executive summary) continue to deliver_report.

Notes: there is no separate `choice` state type. Every branch must live as `conditions` inside the `agentAction` it belongs to. The start step should be a `start` state pointing at `receive_trigger`. Every branching state MUST have a `next` field pointing at the fall-through state. Variables must include each variable named above with its declared dataType.

cases.description: CRON-triggered research run with 5 competitors, good data, medium threat, standard PDF report delivered to team. Full happy path through all phases.
cases.initial_user_text: Run the scheduled competitive research analysis.

cases.description: WebHook trigger with competitor_count=0. Should branch at validate_config to abort_invalid_config.
cases.initial_user_text: Incoming webhook trigger for competitive research.

cases.description: CRON trigger with 25 competitors. Should branch at validate_config to abort_too_many_competitors.
Research discovers critical threat level. Should escalate immediately at check_threat_level to escalate_critical_threat.
cases.initial_user_text: Webhook trigger: urgent competitive scan requested.

cases.description: Manual trigger where user cancels at confirm_parameters. Should route to abort_user_cancelled.
cases.initial_user_text: I want to run a competitive research analysis manually.

turns.user_text: I want to research the AI infrastructure space.
Look at 5 competitors over the last 60 days.
Actually no, cancel the run. I need to rethink the scope.

cases.description: Research gathering finds very few data points (< 10). Should branch at check_data_sufficiency to insufficient_data_report.
cases.initial_user_text: Run the scheduled competitive research for quantum computing.

cases.description: Document processing receives contracts with low extraction confidence (< 60). Should branch at validate_extraction to flag_low_confidence.
cases.initial_user_text: Webhook trigger: process incoming contracts and run competitive analysis.

cases.description: Research finds more risks than opportunities, triggering a defensive report. Distribution scope is board-level, so executive summary is added. Tests the generate_defensive_report and add_executive_summary branches.
cases.initial_user_text: Run the scheduled competitive research for cybersecurity sector.
